titleStmt.title: SII 4.266: original edition by H. Krishna Sastri

No. 266. (A.R. No. 36 and 37 of 1889). ON TWO SLABS At the top of the first slab are cut in a line the figures of the sun, liṅga on a pedestal and the cresent.SET UP TO THE WEST OF THE UDDANA-VIRABHADRASVAMIN TEMPLE IN THE SAME VILLAGE.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Lines 1 to 35 begin with an anusvāra.śubhamastu [.] śrīvīrabhadranē gati(hi) .
namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracāma
racāravē . trailōkyanagarāraṁ
bh(ā)mulastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē .
svasti . śrījayādbhudayaśālivā
hanaśakavaruṣaṁ 1467 neya
viśvāvasusaṁvatsarada jyēṣṭha śu 11
śukradalu śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājā
dhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvī
rapratāpa śrīvīrasadāśivadēva
mahārāyaru vidyānagarada
lli ratnasiṁhyāsanārūḍarāgi pruthvīrā
jyaṁ gaiUttaṁ yiralu sadāśiva
rāyara kā¨ryyake ka¨rttarāda rāmarā
jayyanavara ta(ṁ)mmaṁdiru pradhā
ni Eṟadiṁmarājayya
navara daḷavāyi jaṁgamai
yyanavaru sadāśivadēvama
hārāyarigū rāmarājai
yyanavarigū pradhāni Eṟa
diṁmarājayyanavarigū pu(ṁ)

ṇyavāgabēkeṁdu kṛṣṇāpurada pēṭeya
hiriyakāluveya chatrada baḷiyalū
kaṭistaṁthā muduvīraṁṇana pratiṣṭayanū
māḍi pratiṣṭhākāladalū muduvīraṁṇage
ciṁnada Ābharaṇagaḷu rajatādiUpakara
ṇagaḷanū sama¨rpisi dēvara śrīkā¨ryyagaḷi
ge dīpārādhane phalāpūpādi nityanaivēdyagaḷi
geyū naimittika paṁcapa¨rva rathamahōtsa
va brāhmaṇaviniyōga Aṁgaraṁgavaibhō
gamodalāda viniyōgagaḷigāgi tatkālada
lli sa¨rvamānyavāgi sama¨rpista svāstegaḷu hirisa
tradalu dina 1 kaṁ Aki sidhe hesaru sidhe 1 tu
pa paḍi 1 Eṁṇe paḍi 1 Upu paḍi kāyi
mēlōgara maṇa ÷ hōḷu 16 vīḷyadele
26 niṁbeyahaṇu 3 meṇasu śaṁbhāra
saha kruṣṇāpuradapēṭeyalu mūlevī
sa nānāhēru 1 kke saluUdu kāsu 1 sōma
vāradalu Aṁgaḍi 1 ke saluva kāsu 1
[A]cutāpuradapēṭeyalu mūlevīsa
Oḷahēru 1 ke saluUdu kāsu 1 maṁ
gaḷavāradalū Aṁgaḍi 1 ke saluva kāsu 1 .
Digital edition of SII 4.266 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
